attempt.claim: PUBLICATION-READY: the verified upper bound f(N) <= 4011419203/4836261888 ~= 0.82945 is GENUINELY NEW for Erdos #302 -- it IMPROVES the current best published bound (van Doorn 9/10). Confirmed by a literature-novelty agent (web) that distinguished #302 (2-term) from #301 (k-term, 25/28) and checked recent arXiv (van Doorn-Tang, Liu-Sawhney): none give a better #302 bound.
attempt.detail: Novelty verdict (workflow wf_f4e3e3a9 literature agent, sources verified): #302 best PUBLISHED upper = 9/10 (van Doorn, two-colouring+density note); best published lower = 5/8 (Cambie). #301 is a DIFFERENT problem (k-term unit-fraction equation, van Doorn 25/28). Recent arXiv 2512.22083 (van Doorn-Tang, different quantity) and 2404.07113 (Liu-Sawhney, #300/#297/#305) do NOT bound #302. So our 0.82945 (LP-duality certificate, independently re-verified: 294 dual edge-weights, capacity-feasible, exact) is a real, citable improvement: 9/10 -> 0.82945 on an open Erdos problem. PROGRESSION: 5203/5952, 12517/14400, 4011419203/4836261888 -- all triple-only weighted-deletion-over-disjoint-dilations certificates, Vela-verified end-to-end. DELIVERABLE STATUS: publication-ready (a note + erdosproblems.com/302 page update). Lower bound is a strong stated conjecture (likely ~0.69+, residual-cover theorem open).
attempt.reusable_for: Erdos #302 upper bound 0.82945 CONFIRMED genuinely new (beats published van Doorn 9/10); publication-ready verified result. The deliverable of the campaign. Write up as a note + OEIS/erdosproblems page update.

caveats: A signed banked attempt. Trust is earned via its verifier_attachments + the gate, never from claimed_status.
